exercises.prompt: A 23-year-old barista is serving a customer around her own age in a casual café in Madrid. Which register is most natural today?
You're writing a formal email to your electricity company to dispute a charge. Even though the phone agent chatted with you informally last time, which register belongs in the written complaint?
In an Argentine song you hear the line 'Vos sabés que te quiero.' In Castellano Spanish, vos here corresponds to which pronoun?
Find and fix the mistake — the pronoun and the verb ending don't agree in formality:
exercises.incorrectSentence: Usted tienes una llamada, señor Ruiz.
exercises.correctSentence: Usted tiene una llamada, señor Ruiz.

exercises.prompt: Rewrite this question for a customer you've just met, using the formal register:
Rewrite this casual request as a formal written request suitable for an email to a manager you don't know personally:
exercises.instruction: rewrite as a polite, formal written request using usted

exercises.prompt: Match each situation to the pronoun that fits it best:

pairs.left: A group of close friends in Madrid
The same group of friends, but in Mexico City
A conversation with a friend in Buenos Aires
A formal letter to a government office
Friends chatting in the Canary Islands

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blanks with the correct form of tener for each register:
exercises.passage:
Estimada señora López: Le escribo para preguntarle si {0} un momento esta tarde para hablar de mi proyecto. Muchas gracias.

Hola Carla, ¿{1} un momento esta tarde para ayudarme con el proyecto?

Hola chicas, ¿{2} un momento esta tarde para ayudarme con el proyecto?
